sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance (fukui-reactivity)

• Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, f⁰ and dual descriptor on acrolein; headline value reported here is the highest electrophilic index f⁺.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density-fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry provenance: fixed user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (acrolein.xyz); no further optimisation – single-point charges for the neutral (N), cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states hence notation DFT//user xyz.
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1) for N state; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) for N±1 calculations.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF 2.x for the DFT charge calculations, ASE driver for file handling.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity check status ok, trustworthy true.
  Warnings verbatim from the engine (none omitted):
    – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – common header present
• Live log & files: the run streamed its progress to the standard chemkit live log (`fukui_<timestamp>.out`) in the working directory; results JSON is at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphin89gpb/live.json` and the per-atom plot at `...agent_call_01_plot.png`.

Result

The most electrophilic atom is C4 (index 3) with f⁺ = 0.64232102. The most nucleophilic atom is O1 (index 0) with f⁻ = 0.39388616. Dual descriptor indicates nucleophilic attack preference at C4 and electrophilic preference at O1. All values are method-dependent and computed rather than experimental.
